# UC-23.9.3 · Waste Diversion and Recycling Rate Tracking

## Description

Unifies ninety days of hauler manifest and smart-bin weight events across Rubicon, WM, Republic, Stericycle-class sourcetypes plus `iot:bin_weight`, normalizes mass to kilograms, classifies diversion using a TRUE-aligned rule (excluding incineration from diversion credits unless you extend the predicate), joins EPA WARM-style factors for Scope 3 Category 5 CO2e intensity, rolls up totals and hazardous fractions by `site_id`, and compares diversion performance to `site_targets.csv` so teams see gap-to-target, certification band, and landfill mass leaders in one table for GRI 306, SASB, ESRS E5, CDP C4.5, and ISO 14001 clause-9 monitoring narratives.

## Value

Facilities, sustainability, and procurement leaders see where physical waste actually went—not just invoices—linking operational diversion to tipping-fee economics, zero-waste programme ROI, and upcoming CSRD-class circular-economy tables. Hazardous rows highlight RCRA e-Manifest completeness risk; Scope 3 Category 5 kilograms give climate teams a defensible bridge into UC-23.9.1 when waste-derived emissions must sit beside energy-led tonnes. Legal and EHS stakeholders gain traceability arguments for Basel Annex VII gaps and California SB-1383 organics gaps when regional flags exist in the targets lookup.

## Implementation

Mint HEC tokens for bin gateways and CSV uploads landing `iot:bin_weight`; deploy Add-on Builder modular inputs for each hauler contract with vault-stored secrets; publish `warm_factors.csv` and `site_targets.csv` under controlled lookup workflow; schedule the saved search weekly for leadership and monthly for disclosure packs; wire threshold alerts on `gap_to_target` and `hazardous_pct` to Sustainability and EHS inboxes before any page to executives.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise 9.2 or Splunk Cloud with roles granting `index=business`, lookup read rights for `warm_factors`, `site_targets`, and optional synonym tables; Heavy Forwarder or Cloud bulk tier reachable over TLS to hauler HTTPS endpoints.
• Credential vault aligned with modular-input secrets (CyberArk, AWS Secrets Manager, or Splunk credential stores) plus OAuth2 refresh artefacts for Rubicon and comparable APIs.
• Governance memo locking diversion semantics: TRUE-aligned excludes waste-to-energy from diverted mass unless leadership explicitly overrides; document that stance beside dashboard footers.
• Capacity planning for ninety-day pulls across sourcetypes—manifest payloads average a few kilobytes each while IoT bins may emit five-minute cadence streams.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Stand up Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens scoped to `index=business` and `sourcetype=iot:bin_weight`; configure Pelican, Bigbelly, Compology, or Sensoneo gateways to POST JSON with authoritative `timestamp_utc` mapped into `_time`. Parallel modular-input apps (Splunk Add-on Builder 2962) poll Rubicon pickups at fifteen-to-sixty-minute intervals using persisted high-water marks on `pickup_id` or `LastModified`; replicate pattern for WM (`api.wm.com` manifests subscription key header), Republic `/api/v2/.../manifest`, Stericycle manifests for hazardous streams with extra RBAC because payloads may imply HIPAA-adjacent operational metadata—restrict indexes or field masks accordingly. Ingest Stericycle alongside general MSW feeds so hazardous mass and e-waste certifications stay queryable together. Wire Splunk DB Connect nightly jobs only for invoice lines that reconcile tipping fees—not for diversion numerators unless Procurement demands accrual alignment. Version `warm_factors.csv` with columns `material_type`, `disposal_method`, `mtco2e_per_tonne`, `factor_year`, noting EPA’s June refresh cadence; keep prior-year rows for locked disclosure windows.

Step 2 — Create the saved search and panels
Paste the SPL from the `spl` field into a saved report `waste_diversion_site_scorecard` with a fourteen-hundred-second window or better on the first run to guard long `append` chains. Clone a second copy with `earliest=-365d` for annual TRUE windows when certification teams need entire rolling-year buckets. Extend the diversion predicate deliberately if corporate policy treats waste-to-energy as diversion—encode that as an `eval policy_wte_as_diverted` toggle near the top. For ESRS E5 packaging splits, optionally join EU Waste List codes via lookup `low_code` before the stats stage. Keep factor application conditional: null `mtco2e_per_tonne` should null `scope3_cat5_co2e_kg` rather than silently zeroing, so climate analysts see coverage gaps.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick two pilot sites with active Rubicon or WM subscriptions. Verify against the Rubicon dashboard totals or Waste Management Connected Customer Portal exports that ninety-day landfill and recycling tonnes align within five percent of Splunk `landfill_kg` plus `recycling_kg` after unit conversion so rolled-up masses reconcile with the hauler portal screens invoicing teams trust. For IoT-only cafeterias, compare summed `iot:bin_weight` organics to kitchen scale audit samples. Reconcile hazardous `hazardous_kg` against EPA RCRAInfo e-Manifest counts for the same generator EPA ID; mismatches usually mean paper manifest routes. When DB Connect invoice lines exist, compare hauler tipping sums to manifest-derived cost proxies to catch API holes. Document any deliberate exclusion of C&D demolition spikes so leadership does not confuse project blur with behaviour drift.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish Dashboard Studio tabs: executive headline row for portfolio diversion_rate and WoW delta sourced from summary indexing if desired; regional superintendent page highlighting `gap_to_target`; hazardous-focused slice filtered `hazardous_pct>2`. Route alerts when `gap_to_target` drops below negative five points for four consecutive weekly snapshots; throttle by `site_id`. Archive monthly PDF snapshots into your evidence SharePoint alongside lookup CSV hashes for ISO 14001 auditors.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• Null `scope3_cat5_co2e_kg` across all rows usually means `warm_factors.csv` keys mismatch normalized `material_type` strings—reload lookup definitions after CSV edits and verify lowercase normalization landed.
• API throttles returning HTTP 429 from hauler endpoints during quarter-close reruns indicate scheduler contention—reduce parallelism or widen jitter between modular-input cron expressions.
• Modular-input API timeouts on Stericycle backlog polls strand hazardous manifests until the worker retries—raise client socket timeouts only after confirming firewall PATH MTU.
• Diversion_rate jumps to one hundred percent at small sites signals denominator gaps—confirm `total_kg` truly spans every hauler sourcetype so inactive feeds zero out denominators accidentally.
• Hazardous kilograms collapse after Stericycle credential rotation—check `_internal` ExecProcessor logs on the Heavy Forwarder for HTTP 401 traces tied to modular-input stanza names.
• Bin telemetry flatlines at zero weight yet logistics shows pickups—sensor occlusion or cellular dropout; correlate with gateway last-seen timestamps before opening work orders.
• Latency breaches service-level objectives when IoT bins keep one-minute cadence—pre-aggregate `iot:bin_weight` into a summary index hourly to shrink union fan-out.
• Manifest duplication after ERP replays DB Connect extracts doubles tonnes—enforce deterministic `_dedup` keys (`manifest_id`,`site_id`) upstream or insert `dedup` after union.
• Regional SB-1383 alerts misfire when `region` abbreviations disagree (`CA` versus `California`)—normalize via lookup before compliance predicates.
• Basel Annex VII identifiers absent on international hazardous rows surface as silent landfill totals until customs audits—maintain validator searches keyed on missing `Annex_VII_form_id` when `cross_border_flag=1`.

## SPL

```spl
index=business sourcetype="rubicon:pickup" earliest=-90d
| append [ search index=business sourcetype="wastemgmt:manifest" earliest=-90d ]
| append [ search index=business sourcetype="republic:manifest" earliest=-90d ]
| append [ search index=business sourcetype="iot:bin_weight" earliest=-90d
    | bin _time span=1d
    | stats sum(weight_kg) as weight_kg by site_id, material_type, _time
    | eval disposal_method=case(material_type IN ("cardboard","paper","plastic","metal","glass"),"recycling",
                                 material_type IN ("organics","food"),"composting",
                                 1=1,"landfill") ]
| eval weight_kg=coalesce(weight_kg, weight_lbs*0.453592, weight_short_tons*907.185)
| eval material_type=lower(coalesce(material_type, waste_stream))
| eval disposal_method=lower(coalesce(disposal_method, "landfill"))
| eval is_diverted=case(
    disposal_method IN ("recycling","recycled","composting","composted","reuse","reused","donation","donated","anaerobic_digestion","ad"), 1,
    1=1, 0)
| eval is_hazardous=if(material_type IN ("hazardous","medical","biohazard","chemical","oil","battery","mercury"), 1, 0)
| lookup warm_factors.csv material_type disposal_method OUTPUT mtco2e_per_tonne
| eval scope3_cat5_co2e_kg=if(isnotnull(mtco2e_per_tonne), round(weight_kg/1000*tonumber(mtco2e_per_tonne)*1000, 2), null())
| stats sum(weight_kg) as total_kg,
        sum(eval(if(is_diverted=1, weight_kg, 0))) as diverted_kg,
        sum(eval(if(is_hazardous=1, weight_kg, 0))) as hazardous_kg,
        sum(scope3_cat5_co2e_kg) as cat5_co2e_kg,
        dc(material_type) as material_type_count,
        values(material_type) as materials,
        sum(eval(if(disposal_method="landfill", weight_kg, 0))) as landfill_kg,
        sum(eval(if(disposal_method="recycling", weight_kg, 0))) as recycling_kg,
        sum(eval(if(disposal_method="composting", weight_kg, 0))) as composting_kg
   by site_id
| eval total_tonnes=round(total_kg/1000, 2)
| eval diversion_rate=if(total_kg>0, round(100*diverted_kg/total_kg, 1), null())
| eval landfill_pct=if(total_kg>0, round(100*landfill_kg/total_kg, 1), null())
| eval hazardous_pct=if(total_kg>0, round(100*hazardous_kg/total_kg, 1), null())
| lookup site_targets.csv site_id OUTPUT diversion_target_pct, true_certification_target, business_unit, region
| eval gap_to_target=if(isnotnull(diversion_target_pct), round(diversion_rate - tonumber(diversion_target_pct), 1), null())
| eval true_certification_status=case(
    diversion_rate>=100, "Platinum",
    diversion_rate>=99, "Gold",
    diversion_rate>=95, "Silver",
    diversion_rate>=90, "Bronze",
    1=1, "Not certified")
| sort - landfill_kg
| table site_id, business_unit, region, total_tonnes, diversion_rate, diversion_target_pct, gap_to_target, true_certification_status, hazardous_pct, cat5_co2e_kg
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio layout: stacked bar chart of waste-stream composition (material_type buckets) by site_id; paired bar chart of diversion_rate by site with a reference line from diversion_target_pct; trend line of rolling ninety-day diversion_rate by region (from site_targets.csv); headline single-value panel for portfolio diversion_rate with week-over-week delta and summed total_tonnes; heatmap of site_id × material_type with colour by sum(weight_kg) to spotlight contamination-driven outliers; optional drill-in table listing materials[] when material_type_count climbs.
